hub_subtitle: Medical English to welcome guests and present with confidence

lesson.menu_title: Writing a Formal Email -- Invitations & Confirmations
lesson.menu_desc: Write a polite formal email: a greeting, an opening line, a short body, and a closing. Invite a doctor to a meeting and confirm it -- "I am writing to invite you..." -- "Sincerely, Graziele." -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 14 -- Writing a Formal Email
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Graziele Dias | Aula 14 -- Writing a Formal Email

listenings.text: Hello Graziele. We have a training meeting on Friday at ten. Could you write a formal email to Doctor Lewis, please? Invite her to the meeting. Please attach the agenda, and ask her to confirm. Start with a greeting and finish with Sincerely and your name. Thank you so much.

Hi Graziele, this is Ana, a quick tip about formal emails. A good email has four parts. First, a greeting, like Hello Doctor Lewis. Then, an opening line, like I am writing to invite you. Next, a short body with the day and time. Last, a closing, like Sincerely, and your name. Short, clear, and polite. You can do it!

extra_audio.text: Graziele writes a formal email to Doctor Lewis. First, she writes the greeting: Hello Doctor Lewis. Then, the opening line: I am writing to invite you. Next, the body with the day and time. After that, the request: Could you confirm? Finally, the closing: Sincerely, Graziele.
